What is the FBI’s foreign counterintelligence responsibility?

As the country’s lead counterintelligence agency, the FBI is responsible for detecting and lawfully countering actions of foreign intelligence services and organizations that employ human and technical means to gather information about the U.S. that adversely affects our national interests. Our investigations include foreign and economic espionage, or “spying” activities, that may involve the acquisition of classified, sensitive, or proprietary information from the U.S. government or U.S. companies. 對佢嘅指控

A U.S. citizen who live mainly in Hong Kong and China is suspected to be a spy for Chinese government. His proactive speech of anti-U.S. and support of Chinese Government on social media (refer to the link below) shows he is NO attachment to the principles and ideals of the U.S. Constitution and U.S Government which is the basic requirement as a U.S. citizen. And I also spot he occasionally post unconfirmed message of U.S. Government (C.I.A.) involved in Hong Kong's protesting activities in the past few years which is a serious accusation. With his U.S. citizen identity, it may enhance the reliability of his speech on U.S. Government.
I am here to urge related department to review his and his family's U.S. citizenship. Please find the below information for your reference and stop them from hurting United State.

Involved personnel: Clifton Ko Chi Sum, 6 August 1958, Hong Kong; Eunice Ko, his daughter which worked in Kaiser Permanente as Medical Doctor
Suspicious charge: Treason, spread anti-U.S. and misleading message of U.S. Government, might be his daughter
Reference: https://www.facebook.com/%E9%AB%98%E5%BF%97%E6%A3%AE-204990132851647/
